title: Cockpit Integration: multi-tenant engine surface for the cockpit UI
description: Transform agent-bober from a single-project, single-run CLI/MCP tool into the engine for a long-lived cockpit backend that drives N parallel runs across M projects. The cockpit consumes agent-bober purely over MCP; this spec ships the engine-side surface: multi-run RunManager with disk persistence and crash recovery, worktree adapter for parallel runs in isolated git worktrees, event-stream MCP tool for live progress, MCP wrappers for careful-flow approvals and project/spec discovery, and MCP wrappers for the vision-era incident/rollback/postmortem/playbook surface that today is CLI-only. Backward compatible: existing 17 MCP tools and all CLI subcommands keep working unchanged.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Run state persistence shape: one JSON file per run at `.bober/runs/<runId>/state.json` (mirrors `.bober/incidents/<id>/` and `.bober/audits/<runId>.jsonl` conventions). State file schema: { runId, task, status, startedAt, completedAt?, progress, result?, error?, projectRoot, branch?, worktreePath?, mode: 'autopilot'|'careful', specId? }. RunManager loads all `.bober/runs/*/state.json` on construction so a crashed/restarted bober process can enumerate runs (status of orphans is reconciled to 'failed' with error='orchestrator crashed' if status was 'running' at load time).

Backward compatibility for the existing singleton RunManager: keep the module-scoped `runManager` export. The new `RunManager` class adds `startRun(task, projectRoot, config, opts?)` returning runId, `getRun(runId)`, `listActiveRuns()`, `abortRun(runId, reason)`. The legacy `isRunning()` returns true when ANY run is active (preserves the existing bober_run/bober_status guard). The legacy `getStatus()` returns the newest run for back-compat. New tools key explicitly by runId.

Event stream transport: use MCP server-initiated notifications via `server.notification({ method, params })` — the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk Server supports this directly. Define a custom method name `bober/events` and emit one notification per new history.jsonl/telemetry line whose runId matches the subscription. Subscriptions tracked in-process; on bober restart they're lost (the cockpit must re-subscribe). Backpressure: a per-subscription bounded queue (default 1000 events) drops oldest with a `bober/events.dropped` notification when full.

Worktree adapter scope: ships as a new CLI subcommand `bober worktree run <task>` and an internal helper `runInWorktree(task, projectRoot, config, opts)` used by the cockpit-facing MCP variant `bober_run_in_worktree`. Worktree root defaults to `<projectRoot>/.bober/worktrees/<runId>` (overridable via `pipeline.worktreeRoot` config). Lifecycle: on success, removes worktree if `pipeline.cleanupWorktreeOnSuccess !== false` (default true); on failure, always retains the worktree and prints its path. Dirty trees rejected unless `--allow-dirty` is passed; this mirrors `git worktree add`'s default safety.

Discovery tool scope: `bober_list_projects(searchRoots[])` walks each root one level deep, returning every directory containing `bober.config.json` (matches src/cli/commands/onboard.ts discovery pattern). `bober_list_specs(projectPath)` reads `.bober/specs/*.json` and returns { specId, title, status, sprintCount, completedAt? } per file. `bober_get_project_state(projectPath)` returns { configExists, activeRunCount, lastRunAt?, openIncidentCount, pendingApprovalCount, specCount }. All three tools accept absolute paths only; relative paths are rejected with a clear error to avoid cwd-coupling bugs.

assumptions: The cockpit backend mounts agent-bober as a long-lived MCP server (likely via `agent-bober mcp` stdio transport spawned per project root, or one server-per-cwd). The cockpit handles credential injection via env vars; agent-bober does not learn about credentials. Evidence: src/mcp/server.ts:55-58 takes projectRoot as a constructor arg, confirming the per-project-root model.

The existing 17 MCP tools' input/output JSON schemas remain stable — the cockpit and other MCP clients (Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code) may already depend on them. New tools are purely additive. Evidence: src/mcp/tools/index.ts:24-45 enumerates and version-locks the 17 tools; the registry pattern at src/mcp/tools/registry.ts:39-47 supports additive registration.

MCP server-initiated notifications are well-supported by @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. The Server class exposes a notification() method usable from tool handlers. The cockpit will run an MCP client that subscribes to a notification method name we define. Evidence: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk patterns are documented and used in src/mcp/server.ts; transport is StdioServerTransport which supports server-initiated frames.

Run state persistence uses one directory per run at `.bober/runs/<runId>/` so callers can stream additional per-run artifacts (logs, generator transcripts) without polluting `.bober/runs/`. Evidence: `.bober/incidents/<id>/` already follows this directory-per-entity pattern at src/incident/timeline.ts:97-99.

Worktree creation uses raw git CLI (`git worktree add <path> <branch>`) via the existing utils/git module rather than a new library dependency. The branch naming follows the existing `generator.branchPattern` config (`bober/{feature-name}`). Evidence: src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:57 imports from utils/git; existing branch flow is bober/<feature>.

Crash recovery is best-effort: a `running` state at load time is reconciled to `failed` with error='orchestrator crashed' (or similar). True resume-where-we-left-off is OUT OF SCOPE — the cockpit may re-submit the task. Documented in the run state schema.

Disk approvals already work cross-process via `.bober/approvals/<id>.pending.json` (Sprint 9 of bober-vision). The MCP wrappers simply call the same filesystem helpers (state/approval-state.ts) the CLI uses. No orchestrator coupling. Evidence: src/cli/commands/approve.ts:33-83 + src/state/approval-state.ts.

Vision-era MCP wrappers reuse existing TS modules: src/incident/timeline.ts (createIncident, listIncidents, setIncidentStatus), src/incident/orchestrator.ts (abort, readIncidentMetadata), src/incident/rollback.ts (planRollback, executeRollback), src/incident/playbook-search.ts (loadPlaybooks, searchPlaybooks). No business logic moves; the MCP layer is a thin adapter. Evidence: src/cli/commands/incident.ts:23-36 + src/cli/commands/playbook.ts:21-28 import these helpers directly.

Event subscriptions are in-memory; on bober MCP server restart, the cockpit must re-subscribe. This is acceptable because the cockpit is the long-lived process and bober restarts are rare. Persisted run state still survives restart.

All new MCP tools follow the existing pattern: zod-friendly JSON-schema input, JSON-stringified output via `JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)`, throws McpError for protocol-level errors, returns `{ error: '...' }` JSON for user-facing soft errors. Evidence: src/mcp/tools/run.ts:36-91 + src/mcp/tools/status.ts:29-99.

An end-to-end 'fake cockpit client' test in the final sprint exercises every new MCP tool against a real MCP server subprocess speaking stdio JSON-RPC. Pattern: spawn `node dist/cli.js mcp` as a child process, send JSON-RPC requests, assert responses. Mirrors tests/e2e/four-modes.test.ts which spawns ExternalMcpServer subprocess for the MCP protocol boundary (bober-vision Sprint 27).

The cockpit-integration spec does NOT introduce credential management, auth, or deployment skills. Cockpit handles credentials. Deployment skills are a separate parallel spec. Scope-policed in outOfScope below.
outOfScope: The cockpit UI itself (separate repo, separate team).
The cockpit backend that mounts MCP servers (separate repo, separate team).
Credential storage (AWS/Railway/Cloudflare keys) — cockpit holds credentials and injects them as env vars when invoking bober. Bober has no awareness of secrets management.
Deployment provider skills (EC2/Railway/Cloudflare adapters) — separate spec, runs in parallel.
Discussion-agent / chat memory layer between user and bober — belongs in the cockpit, not in bober.
Multi-user auth / RBAC / billing inside bober — cockpit owns those.
Persistent event subscriptions surviving bober restart — cockpit re-subscribes after detecting a restart.
True resume-where-it-crashed for interrupted runs — crash recovery marks as failed only; cockpit may re-submit.
Cross-project parallel run orchestration policy (rate limiting, queueing across projects) — cockpit decides when to spawn runs; bober just runs them.
Changing the existing 17 MCP tools' input/output schemas — strictly additive.
Replacing the StdioServerTransport with HTTP/WebSocket transports — stdio remains the supported transport.
Auto-detecting cockpit vs non-cockpit consumers — the engine behaves the same regardless of who is calling.
Cross-worktree merging / conflict resolution — each worktree is independent; cockpit handles any merging via standard git workflows.

features.title: Multi-run RunManager with disk persistence and crash recovery
features.description: Refactor RunManager from singleton-with-single-activeRun to a keyed map of RunStates, persisted to `.bober/runs/<runId>/state.json`. RunManager.load() enumerates state files on startup. Running-but-orphaned runs are reconciled to failed/orchestrator-crashed. Existing isRunning()/getStatus() preserved as compatibility shims.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `RunManager` exports `startRun`, `getRun(runId)`, `listActiveRuns()`, `abortRun(runId, reason)`, `load(projectRoot)` as new methods alongside the existing `isRunning()`/`getStatus()` shims.
AC2: Each new run creates `.bober/runs/<runId>/state.json` synchronously before returning. State is updated on every progress/result/error transition (atomic temp-file+rename to avoid torn writes).
AC3: On `load()`, any state file with status='running' is reconciled to status='failed' with error='orchestrator crashed before completion'.
AC4: `bober_run` and `bober_status` continue to work unchanged from the external caller's perspective; legacy `isRunning()` returns true if any run is active.
AC5: Existing 1116 tests all pass.

features.title: Multi-run MCP surface: list/get/abort by runId
features.description: New MCP tools `bober_list_active_runs`, `bober_get_run_status(runId)`, `bober_abort_run(runId, reason)`. Built on the feat-1 RunManager. Replaces the singleton-status assumption with explicit runId addressing.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober_list_active_runs` returns `[{ runId, task, status, startedAt, projectRoot, specId?, progress }]` for all runs known to RunManager (not just active — name retained for cockpit clarity; filter via status field).
AC2: `bober_get_run_status(runId)` returns full RunState as JSON. Returns `{ error: 'Run not found: <runId>' }` JSON for unknown runIds (not McpError — soft error per existing tool convention).

AC3: `bober_abort_run(runId, reason)` flips state to status='aborted' and writes an abort marker to the run state file. If runId is not currently running, returns `{ error: 'Run is not active' }` JSON.
AC4: All three tools registered via the standard registerTool pattern; appear in `tools/list`.

features.title: Event stream MCP tool with backpressure
features.description: New MCP tool `bober_subscribe_events(runId)` that establishes a subscription. The server emits `bober/events` notifications (server-initiated MCP notifications) per matching event from `.bober/history.jsonl` and `.bober/telemetry/<date>.jsonl`. Per-subscription bounded queue (default 1000) drops oldest and emits `bober/events.dropped` when full.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober_subscribe_events({ runId, since? })` returns immediately with `{ subscriptionId, status: 'subscribed' }` JSON.
AC2: New lines appended to history.jsonl or telemetry/<date>.jsonl whose runId matches are emitted as `bober/events` notifications. Lines without runId fields are filtered out (no broadcast).
AC3: When the in-process queue for a subscription exceeds the bound, oldest events are dropped and one `bober/events.dropped` notification is sent with `{ subscriptionId, dropped: N }`.
AC4: `bober_unsubscribe_events({ subscriptionId })` cleanly closes the subscription and stops file-watch handles.

features.title: Worktree adapter for parallel runs
features.description: `bober worktree run <task>` CLI subcommand + internal `runInWorktree(task, projectRoot, config, opts)` helper. Creates a git worktree under `.bober/worktrees/<runId>` (or configured root), runs the pipeline inside it, reports back the worktree path + branch + runId. Cleanup-on-success configurable; retain-on-failure always. Dirty trees rejected unless `--allow-dirty`.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober worktree run <task>` registered as a CLI subcommand. Prints `{ runId, branch, worktreePath, projectRoot }` JSON on completion.
AC2: Worktree created via `git worktree add` from main branch (or configurable base). Branch name derived from `generator.branchPattern` config (default `bober/<slug>`).
AC3: On pipeline success, worktree is removed via `git worktree remove` when `pipeline.cleanupWorktreeOnSuccess !== false`. On failure, always retained and path printed to stderr.
AC4: Dirty working tree rejected with exit code 1 and a clear error unless `--allow-dirty` is passed.
AC5: RunState records the worktreePath and branch so `bober_get_run_status` exposes them to the cockpit.

features.title: Careful-flow MCP wrappers
features.description: New MCP tools `bober_list_pending_approvals(projectPath?)`, `bober_approve_checkpoint({ checkpointId, projectPath?, edits? })`, `bober_reject_checkpoint({ checkpointId, projectPath?, feedback })`. Thin wrappers around the existing src/state/approval-state.ts + src/cli/commands/{approve,reject,list-approvals}.ts logic.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober_list_pending_approvals` returns the same data shape as `bober list-approvals --json` (array of `{ checkpointId, ageMs, prompt }`).
AC2: `bober_approve_checkpoint` writes `.bober/approvals/<id>.approved.json` with the same payload shape as the CLI. Returns `{ approvedAt, checkpointId }` on success.
AC3: `bober_reject_checkpoint` writes `.bober/approvals/<id>.rejected.json` with `{ feedback, rejectedAt }`. Feedback is required (non-empty string).
AC4: All three tools accept an optional `projectPath` arg; default to `cwd()` when omitted. Reject relative paths to avoid cwd-coupling bugs.

features.title: Project + spec discovery MCP
features.description: New MCP tools `bober_list_projects({ searchRoots[] })`, `bober_list_specs({ projectPath })`, `bober_get_project_state({ projectPath })`. Enables a project-list sidebar in the cockpit.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober_list_projects({ searchRoots: [...] })` walks each root one level deep, returns `[{ projectPath, name, mode, hasActiveRuns, lastRunAt? }]` for every directory containing `bober.config.json`.
AC2: `bober_list_specs({ projectPath })` returns `[{ specId, title, status, sprintCount, completedAt? }]` reading from `.bober/specs/*.json`.
AC3: `bober_get_project_state({ projectPath })` returns `{ configExists, activeRunCount, lastRunAt?, openIncidentCount, pendingApprovalCount, specCount, mode? }`.
AC4: All three tools accept absolute paths only; relative paths return `{ error: 'projectPath must be absolute' }` JSON.

features.title: Vision-era MCP wrappers
features.description: Eight new MCP tools wrapping the vision-era incident/rollback/postmortem/playbook surfaces: `bober_incident_start`, `bober_incident_status`, `bober_incident_list`, `bober_incident_abort`, `bober_rollback_start`, `bober_postmortem_get`, `bober_playbook_list`, `bober_playbook_search`. Thin adapters over existing src/incident/* modules.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: All eight tools registered, each delegating to the corresponding helper in src/incident/{timeline,orchestrator,rollback,playbook-search,postmortem}.ts.
AC2: Each tool returns JSON with the same data shape the corresponding CLI subcommand emits (e.g. `bober incident list` JSON shape).
AC3: `bober_rollback_start({ incidentId, projectPath })` invokes planRollback then executeRollback; returns `{ planned, executed, escalated?, remaining? }`.
AC4: `bober_postmortem_get({ incidentId, projectPath })` returns the raw postmortem.md content + parsed structured metadata.

features.title: End-to-end fake-cockpit-client integration test
features.description: tests/e2e/cockpit-integration.test.ts spawns `node dist/cli.js mcp` as a child subprocess, speaks JSON-RPC over stdio, and exercises every new MCP tool against real state on a fixture project. Acts as the correctness gate for the cockpit-integration spec.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: tests/e2e/cockpit-integration.test.ts exists. Sets up a tmpdir fixture, spawns the MCP server subprocess, performs JSON-RPC handshake, exercises every new tool.
AC2: Lists active runs initially returns [], then starts a run via bober_run (or runInWorktree), then lists shows the run present with correct runId.
AC3: Subscribes to events for the new runId, asserts at least one bober/events notification arrives within the test timeout.
AC4: Exercises the careful-flow MCP tools: lists pending approvals, approves one, rejects one (with feedback), verifies the disk markers and downstream pipeline behavior.
AC5: Exercises the vision-era MCP tools: starts an incident, lists it, aborts it. Reads back the same incident via the status tool.

AC6: Total runtime under 90 seconds (excluding any LLM-spawning paths — those are mocked at the spawn-mechanism level via the same BOBER_TEST_DETERMINISTIC pattern used in tests/e2e/four-modes.test.ts).
AC7: All 1116 existing tests still pass; new test additions pass deterministically.

integrationPoints: Cockpit backend (external) consumes agent-bober via MCP stdio transport
Git CLI for worktree management (no new npm dependency)
Existing .bober/ disk artifacts (history.jsonl, telemetry/, approvals/, incidents/, specs/, contracts/)
techNotes.dataModel: RunState (new): one JSON file per run at .bober/runs/<runId>/state.json. Schema includes runId, task, status, startedAt, completedAt, progress, result, error, projectRoot, branch, worktreePath, specId, mode. Backward compat with existing in-memory RunState shape via shared TypeScript type.
securityConsiderations: MCP tools accepting projectPath must reject relative paths to prevent cwd-coupling bugs that could write to unintended directories
Worktree creation must respect git's clean-tree safety unless explicit --allow-dirty
Event stream notifications must not leak secrets present in env vars or task descriptions; filter sensitive substrings in a future hardening pass (documented as follow-up, not in this spec)
No new credential handling — cockpit owns credentials

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: RunManager.load() must complete in under 500ms for up to 100 run state files (typical cockpit-managed project count).
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test loads 100 synthetic state files and asserts load duration.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Run state writes must be atomic (temp-file + rename) so a crash mid-write does not leave a torn state.json.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Code inspection + unit test that simulates write interruption.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: All new MCP tools follow the existing pattern (registerTool + zod-friendly inputSchema + JSON-stringified return + JSON soft errors).
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Code review against src/mcp/tools/run.ts + src/mcp/tools/status.ts as templates.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Existing 17 MCP tool input/output schemas remain byte-identical to current behavior.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Snapshot-style assertions on tools/list output + existing tool tests must pass unchanged.

constraints: Brownfield: all existing 1116 tests must pass; no breaking changes to existing CLI or MCP surface.
Sprint budget: 6 sprints total; bober.config.json caps maxSprints at 30 — well within budget.
Stack-locked: TypeScript + @modelcontextprotocol/sdk; no new heavyweight dependencies.
Adhere to existing patterns: src/state/ for state-helper modules, src/mcp/tools/ for MCP tools, src/cli/commands/ for CLI subcommands.
Telemetry guarantees from bober-vision (no network egress; mode-0600 JSONL only) must remain enforceable; new event stream is in-process MCP only.
